skill.name: env-and-assets-bootstrap
skill.description: Environment and assets sub-skill for README-first AI repo reproduction. Prepares conservative conda-first environment, checkpoint and dataset path assumptions, cache location hints, and setup notes before any run on a README-documented repository.
skill.summary: Bootstraps AI research environments with conda-first setup and asset path planning for paper reproduction.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 21 patterns but most are false positives. External command execution via subprocess.run is legitimate package management (conda, pip, venv) with hardcoded arguments. Shell constructs are standard POSIX patterns for environment detection. No malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection detected.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: Python subprocess.run used to execute conda, pip, and venv commands. Arguments are hardcoded strings for package management, not user input injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of subprocess.run with hardcoded conda/pip/venv commands - legitimate package management pattern with no user input injection vector.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution
low_findings.description: POSIX shell command substitution $(cd ... && pwd) used to determine script directory. Standard and safe pattern for cross-platform shell scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard POSIX shell pattern for getting absolute script path - no security risk as arguments are not user-controlled.

content.user_title: Bootstrap AI Research Environments and Assets
content.value_statement: Setting up AI research environments for paper reproduction is complex and error-prone. This skill automates conservative conda-first environment creation and asset path planning to reduce setup friction.
actual_capabilities: Detects and parses environment.yml, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, and setup.py files
Creates conda or virtualenv environments with appropriate Python versions
Generates platform-specific activation commands for Windows, macOS, and Linux
Identifies common asset directories (datasets, checkpoints, cache, weights)
Extracts asset URLs and paths from README and configuration files
Produces conservative setup plans with documented risks and unresolved items
limitations: Does not execute actual model training or inference commands
Requires target repository to have README documentation for best results
Asset discovery is limited to text pattern matching in configuration files
Does not validate downloaded checkpoint authenticity or integrity

use_cases.title: AI Paper Reproduction Setup
use_cases.description: Researchers reproducing results from ML papers need quick environment setup without dependency conflicts.

use_cases.title: Repository Onboarding
use_cases.description: New team members need to set up development environments for AI research projects consistently.

use_cases.title: Reproducibility Auditing
use_cases.description: Reviewers verifying paper claims need to quickly establish working environments from public repositories.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Environment Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this AI research repository and create a conda environment setup plan based on the environment.yml or requirements.txt file.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Platform Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate environment setup commands that work on Windows, macOS, and Linux for this repository, including both conda and virtualenv fallback options.

prompt_templates.title: Asset Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan this repository README and config files to identify all required checkpoints, datasets, and model weights with their download URLs and target paths.

prompt_templates.title: Full Reproduction Preparation
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a complete reproduction plan including environment setup, asset acquisition, and setup verification steps for this AI paper repository.

output_examples.input: Repository with environment.yml declaring name: ml-research
output_examples.output: Environment file detected: environment.yml. Setup command: conda env create -f environment.yml. Activation: conda activate ml-research
Fallback to virtualenv: python -m venv .venv, then pip install -r requirements.txt. Activation commands provided for all platforms.

best_practices: Always review generated setup plans before executing commands in your environment
Use dry-run mode to preview commands without making system changes
Document any unresolved dependencies or missing assets for later investigation
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for repositories without README or environment files
Do not execute generated commands without understanding their effects
Do not rely solely on automated asset discovery for critical reproducibility work

faq.question: What Python version does this skill use?
faq.answer: Defaults to Python 3.10 but configurable via --python-version argument. Uses the version specified in environment.yml if available.

faq.question: Does this skill work without conda installed?
faq.answer: Yes. Falls back to Python virtualenv and pip when conda or mamba are not available on the system.

faq.question: Can this skill download checkpoints automatically?
faq.answer: No. It only identifies asset locations and URLs from documentation. Download must be performed separately by the user.

faq.question: How does it handle Windows compatibility?
faq.answer: Generates platform-specific activation commands for PowerShell on Windows and bash scripts for macOS and Linux.

faq.question: What if my repository has no environment file?
faq.answer: Creates a minimal virtualenv setup plan and lists unresolved dependencies that require manual resolution.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to run on untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: The skill only reads files and generates plans. Always review generated commands before execution, especially for untrusted code.
